display.purpose: First-screen hero and highest-impact identity statements.

headline.purpose: Large editorial section transitions and major content anchors.

title.purpose: Section-card headings, project names, and strong interior emphasis.

body.purpose: Dense explanatory content for hiring evaluation.

label.purpose: Indices, utility labels, and scanning aids.

shadows.name: soft-ring
shadows.purpose: Default containment for panels and reusable cards.

shadows.name: floating-surface
shadows.purpose: Compact elevated controls and smaller utility surfaces.

shadows.name: section-shell
shadows.purpose: Major dossier shells and hero-adjacent containers.

motion.name: state-transition
motion.purpose: Default button, chip, border, and state transitions.

motion.name: hero-stage-transition
motion.purpose: Reserved baseline timing for future interactive hero affordances.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: White pill CTA for direct hiring actions such as email and CV download.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; min-height: 3rem; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 0.625rem; border: 1px solid rgba(97,97,97,0.46); border-radius: 999px; background: #f4f4f4; color: #121212; padding: 0.875rem 1.25rem; font: 500 0.98rem/1 Manrope, "Segoe UI", sans-serif; box-shadow: 0 0 0 1px rgba(240,240,240,0.16); transition: transform 200ms ease-out, background 200ms ease-out; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { transform: translateY(-0.125rem); background: #ededed; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #f5f5f5; outline-offset: 4px; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Dark frosted pill action for supporting links and external profile actions.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { display: inline-flex; min-height: 3rem; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 0.625rem; border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.1); border-radius: 999px; background: rgba(38,38,38,0.72); color: #f4f4f4; padding: 0.875rem 1.25rem; font: 500 0.98rem/1 Manrope, "Segoe UI", sans-serif; backdrop-filter: blur(10px); transition: transform 200ms ease-out, border-color 200ms ease-out, background 200ms ease-out; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { transform: translateY(-0.125rem); border-color: rgba(255,255,255,0.18); background: rgba(48,48,48,0.88); } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #f5f5f5; outline-offset: 4px; }

components.name: Chip
components.description: Compact metadata chip for stack, strengths, and quick facts.
components.css: .ds-chip { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.5rem; border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.1); border-radius: 999px; background: rgba(38,38,38,0.72); color: #bdbdbd; padding: 0.375rem 0.75rem; font: 400 0.875rem/1.4 Manrope, "Segoe UI", sans-serif; }

components.name: Section Dossier Card
components.description: Primary structured reading unit for portfolio sections after the hero stage.
components.html:
01 / OVERVIEW

Frontend systems, product polish, and delivery.

High-signal summary copy for a hiring evaluator.
components.css: .ds-section-card { min-width: 0; border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.08); border-radius: 1.6rem; background: rgba(21,21,21,0.92); color: #f4f4f4; padding: 1.5rem; box-shadow: 0 0 0 1px rgba(244,244,244,0.1), inset 0 0.5px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.04), 0 28px 60px rgba(0,0,0,0.34); } .ds-section-card .ds-label { margin: 0 0 1rem; color: rgba(148,148,148,0.86); font: 400 0.72rem/1 "IBM Plex Mono", ui-monospace, monospace; letter-spacing: 0.22em; text-transform: uppercase; } .ds-section-card h2 { margin: 0; max-width: 14ch; font: 600 clamp(1.7rem, 4.1vw, 2.8rem)/0.98 "Space Grotesk", Manrope, sans-serif; letter-spacing: clamp(-0.05rem, -0.18vw, -0.11rem); } .ds-section-card p { margin: 1rem 0 0; max-width: 62ch; color: #bdbdbd; font: 400 1rem/1.75 Manrope, "Segoe UI", sans-serif; }

components.name: Hero Stage
components.description: Reserved first-screen canvas stage for a future interactive hero with optional sound.
components.html:
Frontend Engineer

Alex Guseynov

Interactive hero planned here
components.css: .ds-hero-stage { position: relative; min-height: 100svh; overflow: hidden; border-radius: 1.35rem; background: radial-gradient(circle at 50% 30%, rgba(255,255,255,0.06), transparent 24%), #050505; color: #f4f4f4; } .ds-hero-stage canvas { position: absolute; inset: 0; width: 100%; height: 100%; } .ds-hero-copy { position: relative; z-index: 1; display: flex; min-height: 100svh; flex-direction: column; justify-content: flex-end; gap: 1rem; padding: 1.5rem; background: linear-gradient(180deg, rgba(5,5,5,0.08), rgba(5,5,5,0.82)); } .ds-hero-copy p { margin: 0; color: rgba(148,148,148,0.86); font: 400 0.72rem/1 "IBM Plex Mono", ui-monospace, monospace; letter-spacing: 0.22em; text-transform: uppercase; } .ds-hero-copy h1 { margin: 0; max-width: 11ch; font: 700 clamp(2.55rem, 8.5vw, 5.85rem)/0.86 "Space Grotesk", Manrope, sans-serif; letter-spacing: clamp(-0.12rem, -0.35vw, -0.24rem); } .ds-hero-copy span { color: #bdbdbd; font: 400 1rem/1.75 Manrope, "Segoe UI", sans-serif; }

narrative.overview:
This portfolio should feel like a senior frontend engineer's dossier rendered as a deliberate interface: dark, restrained, highly legible, and confident enough to let typography and structure do most of the persuasion. It is not a decorative resume page and not a startup landing page pretending to be one.

The system is grayscale by design. Pitch black background, white and gray surfaces, crisp type hierarchy, and subtle separation are the primary tools. Weight, line, border, and pacing matter more than color. Motion should stay purposeful and sparse, with the first viewport reserved for a future interactive canvas hero that may introduce sound and game-like behavior before the rest of the portfolio content begins.

It explicitly rejects generic template portfolios, overdone SaaS landing page conventions, playful junior developer aesthetics, corporate resume pages, and AI-generated dark glass UI patterns where glow, blur, and cards substitute for judgment.
keyCharacteristics: Pitch black canvas with grayscale surfaces only.
Typography-led hierarchy where font weight and line treatment carry emphasis.
Rounded but controlled containers and tactile pill controls.
Dense-but-readable dossier sections after a more immersive first-screen hero stage.
Motion and sound treated as optional enhancements, never as required comprehension.

rules.name: The Grayscale Commitment Rule
rules.body: Do not solve hierarchy by adding color. Solve it with contrast, weight, spacing, borders, and restraint.

rules.name: The Pitch Black Rule
rules.body: The background stays black or near-black. Do not introduce warm beige, colored bands, blue glows, or novelty gradients.

rules.name: The White Is the Accent Rule
rules.body: White is not the default answer for everything. Its force comes from selective use on CTA fills, active states, and high-priority headings.

rules.name: The Weight Before Ornament Rule
rules.body: If emphasis is needed, try font weight, scale, or line-length contrast before adding decorative treatment.

rules.name: The Hero Compression Rule
rules.body: Tight tracking and compressed line-height belong to display text only. Body copy must remain open, steady, and easy to scan.

rules.name: The Border First Rule
rules.body: Reach for border contrast or a ring before reaching for a larger shadow.

rules.name: The No Decorative Glow Rule
rules.body: Glow, blur, and bloom are not the visual concept. If the future hero uses light effects, they must support the interaction, not replace typography and structure.

dos: Do keep the palette grayscale: pitch black background, white and gray text, and charcoal surfaces only.
Do make hierarchy through font weight, size, line-height, and spacing before inventing decorative treatments.
Do preserve an obvious contact path through email, CV, GitHub, and LinkedIn actions.
Do keep future hero animation and sound optional, interruptible, and respectful of reduced-motion expectations.
Do use specific frontend evidence: React, TypeScript, Vue, UI systems, modernization, testing, and practical AI-assisted delivery.
donts: Don't create a generic template portfolio that feels interchangeable across developers.
Don't use overdone SaaS landing page conventions, including inflated claims, decorative metric blocks, and empty conversion language.
Don't make the site feel like a playful junior developer portfolio.
Don't reduce the site to a corporate resume page or static CV.
Don't use AI-generated dark glass UI patterns where glow, blur, and cards substitute for judgment.
Don't introduce colored side-stripe borders, gradient text, heavy glassmorphism, or identical icon-card grids.
Don't break the grayscale palette with blue accents, warm beige surfaces, purple gradients, or other decorative color additions.
